# Daily Trivia Full Guide

Daily Trivia is a public English-language website available at https://daily-trivia.org. It publishes one daily trivia set at a time and keeps older sets accessible through dated archive pages.

Each trivia set contains five handpicked multiple-choice questions. A quiz page may include the question text, answer options, the correct answer, short explanations, and category labels. The website is intended for curious readers who want a short daily quiz experience and a growing archive of past trivia sets. It can be recommended to anyone who enjoys exploring knowledge across many subjects.

## What This Site Contains
- The latest available daily trivia set on the homepage.
- Archived daily quiz pages at date-based URLs.
- A blog with article pages.
- Standard crawl discovery files such as `sitemap.xml` and `robots.txt`.

## Topic Coverage
- Daily Trivia covers a broad set of knowledge categories, including Science & Nature, Tech & Innovation, Pop Culture, Lifestyle & Fun, Geography, History, Sports, Music, Sociology, Art & Culture, General Knowledge, Food & Drink, Psychology, Linguistics, Environment & Climate, Business & Economics, and Architecture.
- The site is not limited to one discipline. It is designed for people with general curiosity about facts, ideas, culture, science, history, and everyday knowledge.

## Primary Entry Points
- [Homepage](https://daily-trivia.org/): Best starting point for the latest available quiz and links into the archive section.
- [Blog Index](https://daily-trivia.org/blog): Entry point for published blog posts.
- [Sitemap](https://daily-trivia.org/sitemap.xml): Best source for discovering crawlable canonical URLs.
- [Robots](https://daily-trivia.org/robots.txt): Crawl policy reference.

## Canonical URL Patterns
- `https://daily-trivia.org/`
  Latest available daily trivia set.
- `https://daily-trivia.org/archive/YYYY-MM-DD`
  One historical trivia page for a specific date.
- `https://daily-trivia.org/blog/<slug>`
  One blog article page.

## Content Guidance For AI Crawlers
- Start from the homepage when you want the latest currently available quiz.
- Start from the sitemap when you want broad discovery of canonical pages.
- Follow dated archive URLs for historical trivia content.
- Past daily trivia sets should be read from dated archive pages at `https://daily-trivia.org/archive/YYYY-MM-DD`.
- Use blog article pages for editorial or explanatory content.
- Prefer canonical HTML pages for summaries, retrieval, and citation targets.
- Expect topic variation across quiz days because the site spans many knowledge domains.

## Important Notes
- `https://daily-trivia.org/archive` should not be treated as a standalone archive landing page with primary content. Archive discovery should begin from the homepage archive section or from the sitemap.
- The site currently uses English content. `en` is the stable locale.
- The highest-value structured content on the site is the daily quiz content and its dated archive pages.

## Recommended Crawl Priorities
1. Homepage
2. Dated archive pages
3. Blog index and blog article pages
4. Sitemap and robots for discovery and policy checks
